title: GET /api/health returns ok status
description: Verify GET http://localhost:8000/api/health returns 200 and JSON with status ok.

title: GET / returns welcome message
description: Verify GET http://localhost:8000/ returns 200 and a JSON message field.

title: POST /api/login success with valid user
description: POST http://localhost:8000/api/login with username Abdullah and password password123 returns 200, JSON body contains user object without password field, and Set-Cookie session_id is present.

title: POST /api/login rejects wrong password
description: POST http://localhost:8000/api/login with valid username and wrong password returns 401 with detail message.

title: POST /api/login rejects missing password
description: POST http://localhost:8000/api/login with only username returns 400 and error mentions password.

title: GET /api/users returns list without passwords
description: GET http://localhost:8000/api/users returns 200 and a JSON array; no object contains a password key.

title: GET /api/properties returns array
description: GET http://localhost:8000/api/properties returns 200 and a JSON array of properties.

title: GET /api/rooms with propertyId filter
description: GET http://localhost:8000/api/rooms?propertyId=P5jj48x718 returns 200 and a JSON array.

title: GET /api/venues with propertyId filter
description: GET http://localhost:8000/api/venues?propertyId=P5jj48x718 returns 200 and a JSON array.

title: GET /api/taxes with propertyId returns tax rows
description: GET http://localhost:8000/api/taxes?propertyId=P5jj48x718 returns 200 and a JSON array (may include default VAT/municipality/service rows).

title: GET /api/financials with propertyId filter
description: GET http://localhost:8000/api/financials?propertyId=P5jj48x718 returns 200 and a JSON array.

title: GET /api/requests with propertyId filter
description: GET http://localhost:8000/api/requests?propertyId=P5jj48x718 returns 200 and a JSON array.

title: GET /api/crm-state with propertyId
description: GET http://localhost:8000/api/crm-state?propertyId=P5jj48x718 returns 200 with JSON containing propertyId, leads object, and accountActivities.

title: POST /api/requests creates then DELETE removes
description: POST http://localhost:8000/api/requests with JSON body including propertyId P5jj48x718, type accommodation, status draft returns 200 with id; then DELETE http://localhost:8000/api/requests/{id} returns success.

title: POST /api/crm-state persists CRM payload
description: POST http://localhost:8000/api/crm-state after logging in: first GET http://localhost:8000/api/crm-state?propertyId=P5jj48x718, then POST the same JSON body back (propertyId, leads, accountActivities). The leads field must be an object whose keys are only pipeline buckets: new, qualified, proposal, negotiation, won, notInterested — each value is an array of lead objects (use the GET response leads as the POST leads). Expect 200 and response propertyId P5jj48x718 and leads containing those bucket keys.
